title: argemone munita var. rotundata
intro: Argemone munita var. rotundata, also known as Mexican prickly poppy, is a plant species native to the Americas, characterized by its distinctive prickly stems and bright yellow flowers. The plant contains various alkaloids, including sanguinarine, which possess medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions and as a natural insecticide. The health benefits of Argemone munita var. rotundata include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, eczema, and other skin irritations. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in the form of tinctures, infusions, and salves, often used topically to soothe and protect the skin.
